content.text:
Lets start week 6. 
Before we start anything, can you pull the prototype from claude design and check where we stand

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- orchestration-abstraction-guard: Specialized reviewer that audits backend code for provider-specific branching that bypasses the OrchestrationProvider abstraction. Use this proactively when reviewing any PR that touches backend/app/orchestration/, backend/app/services/, or backend/app/api/ — especially any code path handling ADF or Airflow events, trigger bindings, or pipeline_runs. Also invoke when the user asks "does this go through the abstraction?" or similar. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered for technical precision and high-stakes decision-making. It embodies a **Corporate/Modern** aesthetic that prioritizes clarity, reliability, and data density without sacrificing airiness. The brand personality is that of a "Silent Expert"—authoritative but unobtrusive, providing a sophisticated framework for complex data monitoring.

The visual language focuses on a "Data-Driven & Professional" ethos. It uses a disciplined structure, utilizing heavy whitespace to prevent cognitive overload, paired with a sophisticated color palette that directs attention to critical system health indicators. The style avoids unnecessary decoration, instead finding beauty in the alignment, crispness of borders, and the rhythmic regularity of its grid.

## Colors
The palette is anchored by a deep navy and slate foundation to establish a sense of permanence and trust. Vibrant teal serves as the primary action and monitoring accent, distinguishing "system" actions from "data" insights.

- **Primary (Deep Navy):** Used for core navigation, high-level headers, and primary text to ground the interface.
- **Secondary (Slate):** Applied to supporting UI elements, icons, and secondary text to create a hierarchical recession.
- **Tertiary (Vibrant Teal):** Reserved for interactive elements and "healthy" data state indicators.
- **Semantic Palette:** Functional colors (Success, Warning, Alert) are high-chroma to ensure they remain legible even when used as small indicators or sparklines against neutral backgrounds.

## Typography
This design system utilizes **Inter** for all UI and prose elements. Its tall x-height and systematic design make it ideal for data-heavy applications where legibility at small sizes (e.g., table cells and metadata) is paramount. 

- **Headlines:** Use tighter letter spacing and bold weights to provide strong visual anchors.
- **Data Display:** For raw data values, SQL snippets, or schema paths, **JetBrains Mono** is introduced to provide a clear distinction between technical strings and UI labels.
- **Mobile Scaling:** Headlines scale down by roughly 20% on mobile devices, while body text remains consistent at 16px to ensure accessibility.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **12-column fluid grid** system. To maintain "Technical Sophistication," the design system employs an 8pt spacing rhythm (with 4px increments for micro-adjustments). 

- **Margins & Gutters:** Desktop layouts use 32px outer margins and 24px gutters to allow the content to breathe. 
- **Density:** In data-grid views, vertical spacing can be "compacted" to 8px (sm), while dashboard overview cards use 24px (lg) padding to emphasize key metrics.
- **Responsive Behavior:** On tablet, gutters reduce to 16px. On mobile, the grid collapses to a single column with 16px side margins, prioritizing vertical stackability.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is conveyed through **Tonal Layers** and **Low-Contrast Outlines** rather than heavy shadows. This reinforces the "flat and precise" nature of a data tool.

- **Level 0 (Background):** The base surface uses the neutral slate-tinted white (#F8FAFC).
- **Level 1 (Cards/Containers):** Raised elements use a pure white background with a 1px border (#E2E8F0).
- **Interactive Elevation:** Only upon interaction (hover/active) do elements gain an **Ambient Shadow**—a soft, diffused 10% opacity shadow with a slight navy tint to prevent a "dirty" gray look.
- **Focus States:** High-visibility focus rings use a 2px teal stroke with a 2px offset to ensure clarity for keyboard navigation.

## Shapes
The design system adopts a **Soft** shape language. This subtle rounding (4px/0.25rem) strikes a balance between the clinical coldness of sharp corners and the overly casual nature of fully rounded UI.

- **Components:** Buttons and input fields use the base 4px radius.
- **Large Containers:** Cards and modals may use `rounded-lg` (8px) to soften the large surface areas.
- **Status Pills:** Status indicators (e.g., "Healthy," "Critical") use a full pill-shape (999px) to distinguish them instantly from actionable buttons.

## Components
Consistent component behavior is essential for building user trust in a data quality context.

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use a solid teal background. Secondary buttons use a slate outline. Ghost buttons are reserved for utility actions in tables.
- **Data Tables:** These are the heart of the system. They use 1px slate-200 horizontal dividers, no vertical borders, and a subtle slate-50 hover state.
- **Status Badges:** Use a "Dot + Label" pattern. A vibrant colored dot (Green, Amber, Red) accompanies the text to ensure accessibility for color-blind users.
- **Input Fields:** Use a 1px slate-300 border that transitions to teal on focus. Error states use a red border with a bottom-aligned error message in `body-sm`.
- **Monitoring Indicators:** Progress bars and health gauges use a "Thin Track" design (4px height) to remain unobtrusive within dense dashboard layouts.
- **Cards:** Used for grouping related metrics. They feature a 1px border and no shadow by default, gaining a soft shadow only when they are clickable.","contentType":"text/plain","isBase64":false,"truncated":false}

- **Tertiary (Vibrant Teal):** Reserved for interactive elements and "healthy" data state indicators.
- **Semantic Palette:** Functional colors (Success, Warning, Alert) are high-chroma to ensure they remain legible even when used as small indicators or sparklines against neutral backgrounds.

## Typography
This design system utilizes **Inter** for all UI and prose elements. Its tall x-height and systematic design make it ideal for data-heavy applications where legibility at small sizes (e.g., table cells and metadata) is paramount. 

- **Headlines:** Use tighter letter spacing and bold weights to provide strong visual anchors.
- **Data Display:** For raw data values, SQL snippets, or schema paths, **JetBrains Mono** is introduced to provide a clear distinction between technical strings and UI labels.
- **Mobile Scaling:** Headlines scale down by roughly 20% on mobile devices, while body text remains consistent at 16px to ensure accessibility.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **12-column fluid grid** system. To maintain "Technical Sophistication," the design system employs an 8pt spacing rhythm (with 4px increments for micro-adjustments). 

- **Margins & Gutters:** Desktop layouts use 32px outer margins and 24px gutters to allow the content to breathe. 
- **Density:** In data-grid views, vertical spacing can be "compacted" to 8px (sm), while dashboard overview cards use 24px (lg) padding to emphasize key metrics.
- **Responsive Behavior:** On tablet, gutters reduce to 16px. On mobile, the grid collapses to a single column with 16px side margins, prioritizing vertical stackability.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is conveyed through **Tonal Layers** and **Low-Contrast Outlines** rather than heavy shadows. This reinforces the "flat and precise" nature of a data tool.

- **Level 0 (Background):** The base surface uses the neutral slate-tinted white (#F8FAFC).
- **Level 1 (Cards/Containers):** Raised elements use a pure white background with a 1px border (#E2E8F0).
- **Interactive Elevation:** Only upon interaction (hover/active) do elements gain an **Ambient Shadow**—a soft, diffused 10% opacity shadow with a slight navy tint to prevent a "dirty" gray look.
- **Focus States:** High-visibility focus rings use a 2px teal stroke with a 2px offset to ensure clarity for keyboard navigation.

## Shapes
The design system adopts a **Soft** shape language. This subtle rounding (4px/0.25rem) strikes a balance between the clinical coldness of sharp corners and the overly casual nature of fully rounded UI.

- **Components:** Buttons and input fields use the base 4px radius.
- **Large Containers:** Cards and modals may use `rounded-lg` (8px) to soften the large surface areas.
- **Status Pills:** Status indicators (e.g., "Healthy," "Critical") use a full pill-shape (999px) to distinguish them instantly from actionable buttons.

## Components
Consistent component behavior is essential for building user trust in a data quality context.

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use a solid teal background. Secondary buttons use a slate outline. Ghost buttons are reserved for utility actions in tables.
- **Data Tables:** These are the heart of the system. They use 1px slate-200 horizontal dividers, no vertical borders, and a subtle slate-50 hover state.
- **Status Badges:** Use a "Dot + Label" pattern. A vibrant colored dot (Green, Amber, Red) accompanies the text to ensure accessibility for color-blind users.
- **Input Fields:** Use a 1px slate-300 border that transitions to teal on focus. Error states use a red border with a bottom-aligned error message in `body-sm`.
- **Monitoring Indicators:** Progress bars and health gauges use a "Thin Track" design (4px height) to remain unobtrusive within dense dashboard layouts.
- **Cards:** Used for grouping related metrics. They feature a 1px border and no shadow by default, gaining a soft shadow only when they are clickable.

23:| **Current week** | **Week 5 COMPLETE (18/18)** — exit gate met; transitioning to **Week 6 of 8 (Results dashboard + alerting)**, early credit 2/23. Weeks 1–5 complete |
26:| **Week-5 exit gate** | Async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational. — **MET**: run paths across all datasource types done (Snowflake + flat-file batch + UC), run **progress** + **cancel** APIs shipped + the **live run-progress UI (A3)** consuming them, `error`/`skip` operational statuses landed, orchestration `/pipelines` + gap recovery + **scheduling backend (A7)** + the **scheduled-runs UI (A6)** in (scheduling operational end-to-end), **run-history retention sweep (A8)** done, **run-now panel** shipped. The one roadmap Execution-UI task not built — the **recent-runs audit table** — was deliberately **moved to Week 6** to be built once in its prototype page-home (`/dashboard`), not a gate item. Weeks 1–4 exit gates also met — see [CLAUDE.md §13](../CLAUDE.md). Live warehouse/file run remains the deferred Week-1 smoke. |
27:| **Next milestone** | **Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting** (early credit 2/23: Results page scaffold + severity badges, PR-C1). Up next: health-score stat cards + 7-day trend chart, per-suite pass/fail bars, the prototype **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** landing page (`/dashboard`, hosting the moved-in recent-runs table) — gated on a chart-library decision — plus the `ResultPublisher` alerting seam + Teams notifications. |
238:> The roadmap's 5th Execution-UI task — **recent-runs audit table** — was **moved to Week 6** (counted there). Why: the [DataQ Design System](#week-6--results-dashboard--alerting) prototype makes the **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** (`/dashboard`, the [Week-6 task](#week-6--results-dashboard--alerting)) the page-home for the recent-runs widget, and its row drill-down target is the Week-6 routed run-detail page (`/results/:runId`, [#268](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/268) — promotes `Results.tsx`'s in-page `openRun` state to a route). Building a standalone table on `/results` now and rebuilding it on `/dashboard` in W6 is throwaway work; the dashboard also gates on the chart-library decision first. Deferred to build it **once, in its final home** — see Week 6.
239:- [x] ✅ Run now panel — suite picker, env / datasource, notification target — `RunNowPanel` (`components/runs/`): a **cross-suite** run launcher (modal off a **Run now** button in the Results header), distinct from the suite-detail **Run** button (#245) which runs one suite in context. A searchable suite `Select` filtered to the `owner`/`admin`/`edit` ladder the backend `POST /suites/{id}/run` enforces (view-only suites never appear); a read-only `Descriptions` readout of the picked suite's **env** (tag) + **datasource** (connection name + type) + **target** (one-line `catalog.schema.table` / flat-file `path`, mirroring `suiteTarget.ts`). A targetless suite blocks **Run** with an inline reason rather than a backend 422. On trigger it hands off to the shared `LiveRunProgress` drawer (modal closes; run watched + cancellable). **Notification target ships as a clearly-labelled disabled placeholder** ("coming in Week 6") — no notification backend exists yet (Teams/`ResultPublisher` alert routing is Week 6), so it doesn't jump the gun. Self-contained (owns its data fetch + progress drawer) so the Week-6 UI rework can drop it onto the dedicated Execution page unchanged. 3 component tests (no-runnable empty state, env/datasource/target render + trigger, targetless-suite block).
242:- [ ] 🔵 Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results — **moved to Week 6** (counted there), folded into the Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard (`/dashboard`) where the prototype homes the recent-runs widget. Rationale in the group note above + the Week-6 task.
245:**Week 5 total: 18 / 18 ✅ — COMPLETE** _(exit gate met: async runs with live progress across all datasource types + scheduling operational. Early credit: Celery task runner in PR 4; worker runner-dispatch #146; ADF + Airflow 10-min polling #171; plus `trigger_bindings` CRUD #172 out-of-band; check target-table + dispatch ungate #215 (PR-C0a); manual run trigger + run/result read API (PR-C0b); **Suite Triggers UI #216 (PR #247)**. **This stretch (Phase 1–4):** `error` status #297, flat-file batch + `skip` #298, UC run-path coverage #299, run progress API #301, cancel run + #227 #302, `GET /orchestration/pipelines` #305, gap-recovery beat #307, **scheduling backend (A7)**, **live run-progress UI (A3)**, **scheduled-runs UI (A6)**, **run-history retention (A8)**, **run-now panel**. +3 tasks re-tracked here: check target-table #215 (slipped from W3), Suite Triggers UI #216, and the run-enablement read API (PR-C0b); +1 added: the scheduling backend (A7), the backend the roadmap's scheduled-runs UI task implies. The **manual Run button** (PR #245) + the **run-target editor** (PR #246) also shipped earlier this week. **Closeout cleanup:** profiler `null_fraction` DRY ([#147](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/147), PR #329) + `RUN_BAR_STATUS` refactor ([#317](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/317), PR #328); profiler N+1 perf split to backlog [#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327). **Moved to Week 6:** recent-runs audit table — see the Execution-UI note above + the Week-6 task.)_
249:## Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting
287:**Week 6 total: 2 / 23** _(early credit: Results page scaffold + severity badge colours (PR-C1, the GX-Cloud redesign Phase C); 3 dashboard tasks partially landed — status/provider filters, per-check drill-down, pipeline-runs feed. +6 prototype-adoption tasks added: nav/shell, Dashboard landing page, suite-create page, edit-flows→pages, results drill-down route, error pages. +1 moved in from Week 5: recent-runs audit table — folded into the Dashboard page.)_
412:| Week 6 | 2 | 3 | 18 | 23 |
419:> 178 > 100 because ADR 0004 added Airflow tasks, ADR 0011 added two seam tasks (generic runner dispatch, `ResultPublisher`), ADR 0012 added three Week-3 monitor-kind / metric seam tasks, the W5 run-enablement gaps surfaced in review (check target-table #215, Suite Triggers UI #216), the GX-Cloud-style UI redesign added four UI-shape tasks (dedicated/classified connection + check pages), the **DataQ Design System prototype adoption** added eleven tasks (six Week-6 dedicated-page-flow + Dashboard-landing tasks, five Week-7 backend-gated screens + polish), plus PR-review follow-ups not in the original roadmap. Tracked here for honesty. _(The Week-5 recent-runs audit table was **moved to Week 6** — net-neutral on the total: W5 19→18, W6 22→23.)_

lastPrompt: Lets start week 6.  Before we start anything, can you pull the prototype from claude design and check where we stand

aiTitle: Review week 6 prototype from Claude Design

content.text: Let me read the full Week 6 task list to map prototype → our status.

content.content:
249	## Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting
250	
251	**Exit gate:** Full results dashboard live across all source types; alerts firing with suppression.
252	
253	### Results dashboard (11 tasks — 2/11 ✅)
254	> **Build as an in-app React page** (the GX-Cloud-style redesign Phase C), **not Grafana** — reading Postgres directly would bypass DataQ's per-suite sharing + PII redaction; Grafana is deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on ([ADR 0018](adr/0018-results-surface-and-grafana-deferral.md), accepted). The Week-5 run-enablement backend it gates on is **done**: `runs.py` read endpoints (`GET /runs`, `GET /runs/{id}`, `GET /pipeline_runs`) + `POST /suites/{id}/run` (PR-C0b); [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target (PR-C0a). The `results.metric_value`/`duration_ms` columns were seam-built for these trend charts (ADR 0012).
255	- [x] ✅ **Results page scaffold** (PR-C1) — the in-app `/results` page + sidebar nav (Connections · Suites · **Results** · Profile). **Runs tab**: a runs table (suite name, status, triggered-by, started, duration) with a status filter, each row opening a **run-detail drawer** that drills into the per-check results (check name + expectation, severity tag, `metric_value`, observed value). **Pipeline runs tab**: the orchestration monitoring feed (`pipeline_runs`) with a provider filter. Pure presentation helpers (`resultsFormat.ts` — duration/timestamp formatters, status→colour maps) are unit-tested; `api/runs.ts` mirrors the C0b schemas. Demo seed (`demo_data.py`) now lands runs + results (pass/pass/warn/fail spread) + two pipeline-runs + suite targets so the page has real content. 10 vitest + 2 Playwright (`results.spec.ts`). _The rich widgets below build on this scaffold._
256	- [ ] ⬜ Health score stat cards + 7-day trend chart
257	- [ ] ⬜ Per-suite pass / fail progress bars — warn / fail / critical breakdown
258	- [ ] 🟡 Results filter bar — env, datasource type, suite, date range, status — _run **status** filter + pipeline **provider** filter shipped (PR-C1); env/datasource/suite/date-range pending_
259	- [ ] 🟡 Failed check drill-down — sample failing rows from GX result — _per-check drill-down shipped (PR-C1); **sample rows withheld** pending row-level PII redaction ([#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226), ADR 0018)_
260	- [ ] ⬜ Per-check historical trend chart
261	- [ ] 🟡 Orchestration status panel — pipeline/DAG status, polls every 30s, correlated DQ result — _pipeline-runs feed + status shipped (PR-C1, `/pipeline_runs` tab); 30s auto-poll + DQ-run correlation pending_
262	- [ ] ⬜ Datasource type filter — Snowflake / flat file / Unity Catalog toggle
263	- [ ] ⬜ CSV + PDF export of results
264	- [ ] ⬜ **Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results** _(moved from Week 5 — see that week's Execution-UI note)_ — the cross-suite recent-runs widget on the C0b read API (`GET /runs`), each row deep-linking to the run-detail route. **Why deferred from W5:** its page-home is the Week-6 **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** (`/dashboard`, prototype-adoption task below — which already lists a recent-runs table among its hosted widgets), and its drill-down target is the Week-6 routed run-detail page (`/results/:runId`, [#268](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/268)). Both prerequisites are W6 work, and the dashboard gates on a chart-library decision — so building a throwaway standalone table on `/results` in W5 then rebuilding it on `/dashboard` was avoided in favour of building it once, here. Build it as part of the Dashboard page (or its own component the Dashboard mounts).
265	- [x] ✅ Severity badge colours — green / amber / red / dark red — severity-tier (pass/warn/fail/critical) + run-status tag colour maps in `resultsFormat.ts`, rendered as antd Tags on the runs table + run detail (PR-C1)
266	- [ ] ⬜ Health score weighting — apply warn/fail/critical penalty weights
267	
268	### Alerting (6 tasks — 0/6)
269	- [ ] ⬜ `ResultPublisher` seam — dispatch run outcomes from the post-`execute_run` completion point through a small publisher interface (Teams is the v1 implementation, not a hardcoded call); carry a PII redaction / opt-in policy on `sample_failures` at the seam since it leaves DataQ's trust boundary. ## Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting

**Exit gate:** Full results dashboard live across all source types; alerts firing with suppression.

### Results dashboard (11 tasks — 2/11 ✅)
> **Build as an in-app React page** (the GX-Cloud-style redesign Phase C), **not Grafana** — reading Postgres directly would bypass DataQ's per-suite sharing + PII redaction; Grafana is deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on ([ADR 0018](adr/0018-results-surface-and-grafana-deferral.md), accepted). The Week-5 run-enablement backend it gates on is **done**: `runs.py` read endpoints (`GET /runs`, `GET /runs/{id}`, `GET /pipeline_runs`) + `POST /suites/{id}/run` (PR-C0b); [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target (PR-C0a). The `results.metric_value`/`duration_ms` columns were seam-built for these trend charts (ADR 0012).
- [x] ✅ **Results page scaffold** (PR-C1) — the in-app `/results` page + sidebar nav (Connections · Suites · **Results** · Profile). **Runs tab**: a runs table (suite name, status, triggered-by, started, duration) with a status filter, each row opening a **run-detail drawer** that drills into the per-check results (check name + expectation, severity tag, `metric_value`, observed value). **Pipeline runs tab**: the orchestration monitoring feed (`pipeline_runs`) with a provider filter. Pure presentation helpers (`resultsFormat.ts` — duration/timestamp formatters, status→colour maps) are unit-tested; `api/runs.ts` mirrors the C0b schemas. Demo seed (`demo_data.py`) now lands runs + results (pass/pass/warn/fail spread) + two pipeline-runs + suite targets so the page has real content. 10 vitest + 2 Playwright (`results.spec.ts`). _The rich widgets below build on this scaffold._
- [ ] ⬜ Health score stat cards + 7-day trend chart
- [ ] ⬜ Per-suite pass / fail progress bars — warn / fail / critical breakdown
- [ ] 🟡 Results filter bar — env, datasource type, suite, date range, status — _run **status** filter + pipeline **provider** filter shipped (PR-C1); env/datasource/suite/date-range pending_
- [ ] 🟡 Failed check drill-down — sample failing rows from GX result — _per-check drill-down shipped (PR-C1); **sample rows withheld** pending row-level PII redaction ([#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226), ADR 0018)_
- [ ] ⬜ Per-check historical trend chart
- [ ] 🟡 Orchestration status panel — pipeline/DAG status, polls every 30s, correlated DQ result — _pipeline-runs feed + status shipped (PR-C1, `/pipeline_runs` tab); 30s auto-poll + DQ-run correlation pending_
- [ ] ⬜ Datasource type filter — Snowflake / flat file / Unity Catalog toggle
- [ ] ⬜ CSV + PDF export of results
- [ ] ⬜ **Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results** _(moved from Week 5 — see that week's Execution-UI note)_ — the cross-suite recent-runs widget on the C0b read API (`GET /runs`), each row deep-linking to the run-detail route. **Why deferred from W5:** its page-home is the Week-6 **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** (`/dashboard`, prototype-adoption task below — which already lists a recent-runs table among its hosted widgets), and its drill-down target is the Week-6 routed run-detail page (`/results/:runId`, [#268](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/268)). Both prerequisites are W6 work, and the dashboard gates on a chart-library decision — so building a throwaway standalone table on `/results` in W5 then rebuilding it on `/dashboard` was avoided in favour of building it once, here. Build it as part of the Dashboard page (or its own component the Dashboard mounts).
- [x] ✅ Severity badge colours — green / amber / red / dark red — severity-tier (pass/warn/fail/critical) + run-status tag colour maps in `resultsFormat.ts`, rendered as antd Tags on the runs table + run detail (PR-C1)
- [ ] ⬜ Health score weighting — apply warn/fail/critical penalty weights

Enables post-v1 TestRail / JIRA / Xray publishers as additional subscribers with no re-plumbing _(added per [ADR 0011](adr/0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md))_

lastPrompt: Lets start week 6.  Before we start anything, can you pull the prototype from claude design and check where we stand

aiTitle: Review week 6 prototype from Claude Design

content.text:
I've pulled the prototype and mapped it against our codebase. Here's where we stand.

## The prototype (claude.ai/design → "DataQ Design System")

Last updated 2026-06-18, `projectId 317fec67…`. The two screens that matter for Week 6:

- **`DashboardScreen.jsx`** — the *Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard* landing page we haven't built yet: a 4-up KPI row (Data Integrity Score / Total Anomalies / Active Connections / Avg. Time to Resolution), a dark **Quality Trends** bar chart (pass vs fail, 14d), a **Suite Performance** panel (per-suite health progress bars), and a **Recent Runs** table with row→run-detail drill-down.
- **`ResultsScreen.jsx`** — mirrors our shipped `/results`: 4 MetricCards + Runs / Pipeline-runs tabs, **plus** a routed **`RunDetailScreen`** (per-check results table, status/checks-passed/started/duration cards, and a **CSV/JSON download** menu).

One trap worth flagging: `uploads/DESIGN.md` is the **superseded teal** brief — progress.md explicitly says ignore it for palette. The real spec is `readme.md`, and the actual `.jsx` uses indigo (`--indigo-500`), matching our shipped `theme.ts`. So this is a **structure adoption, not a re-skin**.

## Where we actually stand — Week 6 is 2/23

What's shipped (PR-C1):
- ✅ [Results.tsx](frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx) scaffold — Runs + Pipeline-runs tabs, run-detail **drawer**, status/provider filters, severity badge colours in [resultsFormat.ts](frontend/src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts)
- 🟡 Partial: status/provider filters, per-check drill-down (sample rows withheld pending PII work [#226]), pipeline-runs feed

What the prototype shows but we **don't have**:

| Track | Gap |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | The entire `/dashboard` landing page — KPI cards, Quality Trends chart, Suite Performance bars, recent-runs table |
| **Results** | Run-detail is a **drawer** ([Results.tsx:54](frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx#L54)) — prototype wants a routed `/results/:runId` page ([#268]) with CSV/JSON download |
| **Nav/shell** | No Dashboard entry; login lands on `/connections` not `/dashboard` |
| **Edit flows** | Connection/Suite/Check edit are still drawers — prototype wants dedicated pages |
| **Error pages** | No `ErrorState`-based 4xx/5xx pages |
| **Alerting** | 0/6 — `ResultPublisher` seam, Teams config, suppression/dedup/routing — nothing started |

## The one real blocker

The **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard is gated on a chart-library decision** (antd ships no charts → `@ant-design/charts` vs `recharts`, needs to clear `pnpm audit` + bundle-size and get an ADR-light note). That decision should come first since both the Quality Trends chart and the per-check historical trend depend on it.

Want me to start by making the chart-library call (ADR-light), or would you rather kick off with something that *isn't* chart-gated — the **nav/shell restructure** + **`/results/:runId` route** (promoting the drawer to a page with CSV/JSON download), which together unblock the recent-runs drill-down target?

content.content:
{"method":"get_file","path":"readme.md","content":"# DataQ Design System

A design system for **DataQ** — a single-tenant **data quality monitoring platform** built around Great Expectations (the v1). DataQ runs DQ checks across four datasources (Snowflake, ADLS Gen2, AWS S3, Unity Catalog/Databricks) and observes two orchestration providers (Azure Data Factory, Apache Airflow), surfacing health, runs and anomalies through an in-app React console.

This system captures the **shipped product's** visual language: an **Ant Design** foundation with an **indigo** primary on a soft-gray canvas, white surfaces, hairline borders, and a status model that mirrors DataQ's real severity tiers.

> **Personality:** the "Silent Expert" — authoritative, precise, unobtrusive. Beauty comes from alignment, crisp hairlines and the rhythmic regularity of the grid, not decoration.

---

## Sources

This system was reconstructed from the real product, not invented. Explore these to go deeper:

- **Codebase (source of truth):** [github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ) — React + Vite + Ant Design frontend (`frontend/src/`). The brand palette and shell live in `frontend/src/theme.ts` + `App.tsx`; the status/severity model in `frontend/src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts`; per-datasource visuals in `frontend/src/components/connections/connectionVisuals.tsx`. `CLAUDE.md` is the full project guide; `context/DataQ_platform_roadmap.md` the 8-week roadmap.
- **Figma file:** the attached `DataQ.fig` (14 frames — landing page + console screens). Useful for layout reference. **Note:** the Figma explores a teal "Enterprise Monitor" direction that was *not* shipped — this system follows the **indigo Ant Design** app in code, per the project's actual theme.
- **Uploaded spec:** `uploads/DESIGN.md` — an earlier design brief (teal-oriented); superseded by the shipped indigo theme but useful for spacing/typography intent.

You'll build more accurate DataQ designs by reading the repo above — particularly `theme.ts`, `resultsFormat.ts` and the page components under `frontend/src/pages/`.

---

## Tech stack (match it when writing production code)

| Layer | Tech |
| --- | --- |
| Frontend | **React + Vite + Ant Design** (antd) + Monaco editor |
| Auth | Azure AD (MSAL) |
| Backend | FastAPI · Celery · Great Expectations · PostgreSQL · Redis |
| DQ engine | Great Expectations (GX Core) v1 — every check is a GX *expectation* |

When producing production code for DataQ, use **Ant Design components** and read tokens from `theme.ts` (don't hardcode hexes). The React components in this system are framework-free recreations for *prototypes and mocks* — they mirror the antd look so throwaway artifacts match the product, but production work should use real antd.

---

## Content fundamentals

**Voice — the Silent Expert.** Authoritative, terse, technical. State facts and outcomes; never hype.

- **Casing:** Title Case for page/section titles ("Results", "Pipeline runs", "New Connection"). Sentence case for descriptions and helper text. Status values are **lowercase** to match the data model and antd Tags (`pass`, `warn`, `fail`, `critical`, `succeeded`, `failed`).
- **Person:** address the user as **you** ("where DataQ sends your alerts"); the product is **DataQ** in the third person. Avoid "we".
- **Numbers & precision:** lead with the metric. "94.2% pass rate", "128 anomalies", "2.4h avg. time to resolution". Mono-format raw values, ids, schema paths and expectation names (`expect_column_values_to_not_be_null`, `Snowflake_Prod_01`, `prod.analytics.fct_revenue`).
- **No emoji**, no exclamation marks, no cute error copy. A failure says `Failed (Err 504)` or `Task retry limit exceeded`, not "Oops!".
- **Examples:**
  - ✅ "Identify anomalies before they propagate downstream."
  - ✅ "42 anomalies detected. View details."
  - ❌ "Supercharge your data with revolutionary AI! 🚀"
  - ❌ "Looks like something went a little wonky."

---

## Visual foundations

**Color.** Indigo (`#4f46e5`, indigo-600) is the single brand accent — buttons, active nav, links, selected state. Everything else is a **soft-gray neutral system**: a `#f4f5f7` canvas behind white (`#ffffff`) surfaces, hairline borders at `#e6e8eb`, ink text at `#1f2430`. The "selected" tint is one pale indigo (`#eef0fe`) used everywhere (nav, rows). Restraint is the point — color earns attention, so it's spent only on the accent and on status.

**Status / severity.** DataQ's result-severity model (ADR 0005/0016) maps to Ant Design Tag hues: `pass` → green, `warn` → gold, `fail` → red, `critical` → **magenta** (the top tier), `skip` → gray, `error` → volcano. Run/pipeline execution uses `queued` (gray), `running` (blue), `succeeded` (green), `failed` (red), `cancelled` (gold). Always pair color with a label or dot — never color alone (color-blind safe). Datasource cards carry per-source brand accents (Snowflake `#29b5e8`, ADLS/ADF `#0078d4`, S3 `#ff9900`, Unity Catalog `#ff3621`, Airflow `#017cee`).

**Typography.** **Inter** for all UI and prose, **14px base** (Ant Design). Headlines use Ant's Title scale; page titles are level-3 (24px, semibold). **JetBrains Mono** for anything machine: SQL, schema paths, connection ids, expectation types, ids and metric values. Headings get slightly tight tracking; body stays neutral.

**Spacing & layout.** 8pt rhythm with 4px micro-increments. Content padding 24px; card padding 20–24px; gaps of 16/24. A light fixed sider (220px) + white header (56px) frame a scrolling content canvas. Max content width \~1200px, centered.

**Shape & elevation.** Soft, Ant-aligned radii: **8px** on controls (buttons, inputs, menu items), **12px** on cards and drawers, full pills for status. Depth is **flat** — 1px hairline borders define cards at rest; soft, neutral shadows appear only on hover (cards lift \~1px with a diffused shadow) and on overlays/drawers. No heavy drop shadows, no gradients.

**Surfaces & cards.** White, 1px `#e6e8eb` border, 12px radius, no shadow by default. Interactive cards gain a soft ambient shadow + 1px lift on hover. Tables are the workhorse: tinted header row (`#fafbfc`), horizontal dividers only (no vertical borders), subtle `#fafbfc` row hover.

**Motion.** Quiet and quick — 120–180ms ease on background, color, border and shadow. Hover = subtle background/shadow shift; press = 0.5px nudge. No bounces, no infinite decorative loops. Live/running states may pulse a dot or animate an indeterminate progress stripe.

**Focus.** Keyboard focus = a 2px indigo ring (with offset) or a soft indigo halo on inputs — high-visibility, never removed.

**Dark mode (planned post-v1).** v1 ships **light only**, but the system is built dark-ready. The foundation lives in `tokens/dark.css`: a `[data-theme="dark"]` scope that remaps the **semantic layer** (surfaces, text, borders, status tints, elevation) to a deep-slate palette — set `<html data-theme="dark">` (or scope it to any subtree) and the whole UI flips with **zero component changes**. This works because of one contract every component already follows: **reference semantic tokens** (`--bg-card`, `--text-primary`, `--border`, `--surface-*`, `--status-*-bg`, …), never raw scale hexes. The only hardcoded `#fff` allowed is text/dots/knobs sitting *on top of* a brand or status fill (white on the indigo button, white on a solid status pill) — those stay correct in both themes. Dark surfaces are deep slate (`#0d1117` canvas, `#161b22` cards), not pure black; status hues lift for AA contrast (pass `#3fb950`, fail `#f85149`, critical `#db61a2`) over darkened tint backgrounds; depth shifts from hairline borders to deeper shadows. An optional `prefers-color-scheme` auto-switch is included (commented out) for when the team opts in. When you build new components, keep using the semantic tokens and they inherit dark mode for free.

**Imagery.** The product is chrome-and-data; it leans on its own UI (dashboards, tables, charts) rather than photography. A faint yin-yang watermark (5% opacity) sits bottom-right of the content area.

---

## Iconography

DataQ uses **line icons** from **Ant Design Icons**. The shipped app uses **Ant Design Icons** (`@ant-design/icons` — `CloudOutlined`, `DeploymentUnitOutlined`, `NodeIndexOutlined`, `TableOutlined`, `FolderOpenOutlined`, `InboxOutlined`, etc.), tinted with each datasource's brand color on a soft 12%-alpha tile (see `connectionVisuals.tsx`).

- **In this system's recreations**, icons are the real **Ant Design Icons** (outlined set, MIT-licensed, from `@ant-design/icons` / `ant-design/ant-design-icons`) — the exact glyphs the shipped DataQ app renders. They're inlined (1024 viewBox, filled `currentColor` paths) in `templates/app/Icons.jsx` so screens stay self-contained, exposed as `window.Icon.<name>`. Datasource tiles tint each icon with the source's brand color (see `connectionVisuals.tsx`).
- **No emoji** as UI icons. No multicolor/filled icon systems. Unicode glyphs appear only as incidental marks (e.g. the datasource specimen card).
- The **brand mark** is the two-tone indigo **yin-yang** glyph (`assets/logo/dataq-mark.svg`) — it nods to DataQ's pass/fail, expected/observed duality. Use the lockup (`dataq-logo.svg`) in headers, the standalone mark at small sizes, the reversed lockup on dark backgrounds.

---

## What's in here (manifest)

**Foundations**

- `styles.css` — global entry point (import this one file). It `@import`s everything below.
- `tokens/colors.css` · `typography.css` · `spacing.css` · `elevation.css` · `fonts.css` — CSS custom properties + webfonts. **Inter** and **JetBrains Mono** are self-hosted as variable TTFs in `assets/fonts/` (SIL OFL 1.1) via `@font-face`.
- `guidelines/*.card.html` — foundation specimen cards (Colors, Type, Spacing, Brand) shown in the Design System tab.

**Components** (`components/<group>/` — React, framework-free, styled via tokens)

- `buttons/` — **Button**, **IconButton**
- `forms/` — **Input**, **Select**, **Checkbox**, **Switch**
- `feedback/` — **StatusBadge** (severity + run status), **Tag**, **ProgressBar**, **Avatar**, **ErrorState** (4xx/5xx)
- `data/` — **Card**, **MetricCard**, **DataTable**
- `navigation/` — **SegmentedControl**
- `overlay/` — **Drawer** (right-side panel for forms & detail)

**Templates** (`templates/<slug>/` — copy-and-go starting points; shown in the Templates picker)

- `templates/app/` — interactive DataQ console: a light Ant Design shell (white header + light sider + gray canvas). **Login lands on the Dashboard** (Enhanced Monitoring — KPIs, Quality Trends, Suite Performance, Recent Runs). Sidebar: **Dashboard · Connections · Suites · Results · Profile**; footer: **Admin · Settings · Documentation** (placeholder). A demo "View as" role switch (user menu) gates **Admin** behind a **403** for non-admins.
  - **Full-page flows** (sub-routed like the Figma frames, with back-nav): New Connection → *Select Source* (categorized — **Orchestration** first, then Warehouses/Lakehouses/Cloud Storage) → *Configuration* (spec-driven, `ConnectionFlow.jsx`); New Suite → *Details/Source* (`SuiteFlow.jsx`) → *Define Rules* (`CheckFlow.jsx`); Results → click a run → *Run detail* with per-check results + CSV/JSON download (`ResultsScreen.jsx`). Suites also have a **Share** drawer (`ShareFlow.jsx` — invite by email with view/edit permission). Forms driven by the real `connectionFormSpec.ts` / `expectationCatalog.ts`.
- `templates/marketing/` — the DataQ landing page: sticky nav, hero (with a live product preview built from real components), a features bento, a social-proof strip, a dark CTA closer, and a footer. Sections in `MarketingNav.jsx`, `Hero.jsx`, `Features.jsx`, `MarketingClose.jsx`.
- `templates/error/` — standard **4xx & 5xx** HTTP error pages in the app shell, switchable across 400/401/403/404/429/500/502/503/504, built on the `ErrorState` component.
- Each template loads the system through a sibling **`ds-base.js`** — a consuming project repoints one `base` line at the bound `_ds/<folder>` tree.

**Assets**

- `assets/logo/` — yin-yang mark, primary lockup, reversed lockup, app mark.

**Skill**

- `SKILL.md` — makes this folder usable as an Agent Skill in Claude Code.

---

## Using it

Link the one stylesheet and read components off the compiled bundle:

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">
<script src="_ds_bundle.js"></script>
<script>
  const { Button, StatusBadge, DataTable } = window.DataQDesignSystem_317fec;
</script>
```

For **mocks and prototypes**, copy assets out and build static HTML using these components. For **production DataQ work**, use real Ant Design + read `theme.ts`, and treat this system as the reference for palette, status semantics, voice and layout.

content.text:
I've now pulled and read **every screen** in the prototype (`templates/app/*` + `templates/error/`) plus the canonical `readme.md`, and diffed them against our shipped frontend. Here's the full-scope picture — you're right that the 23-task tracking under-counted it.

## The complete prototype screen inventory vs. where we stand

The prototype's `index.html` registers **13 screens** behind a single shell. Mapping each to our codebase:

| # | Prototype screen | Route | Our status | Action for W6 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **AppShell** (header + sider + footer nav + watermark) | — | [App.tsx](frontend/src/App.tsx) shell exists, but nav = Connections·Suites·Results·Profile | **Restructure**: add Dashboard, footer group (Admin·Settings·Documentation), `/`→`/dashboard` |
| 2 | **DashboardScreen** (KPIs · Quality Trends · Suite Performance · Recent Runs) | `/dashboard` | ❌ **doesn't exist** | **New** — landing page; chart-gated |
| 3 | ConnectionsScreen (card grid) | `/connections` | ✅ [Connections.tsx](frontend/src/pages/Connections.tsx) | Align only |
| 4 | **ConnectionFlow** — Source-select → Config (2 pages) | `/connections/new` | 🟡 [ConnectionNew.tsx](frontend/src/pages/ConnectionNew.tsx) is one page | Verify/adopt the categorized **source-select** step (Orchestration first) |
| 5 | SuitesScreen (two-panel) | `/suites` | ✅ [Suites.tsx](frontend/src/pages/Suites.tsx) | Align only |
| 6 | **SuiteFlow** (New Suite) | `/suites/new` | ❌ create is a **drawer** (`SuiteDrawer`) | **Restructure** drawer → page |
| 7 | CheckFlow (Add Check) | `/suites/:id/checks/new` | ✅ [CheckNew.tsx](frontend/src/pages/CheckNew.tsx) | Align only |
| 8 | **ResultsScreen + RunDetailScreen** | `/results` + `/results/:runId` | 🟡 Results shipped; run-detail is a **drawer** ([Results.tsx:54](frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx#L54)) | **Restructure** drawer → route; add KPI cards + CSV/JSON download |
| 9 | **ProfileScreen** (identity + alert channels) | `/profile` | ❌ routes to placeholder `Home` ([App.tsx:100](frontend/src/App.tsx#L100)) | **New** real content |
| 10 | AdminScreen (Control Center) | `/admin` | ✅ [Admin.tsx](frontend/src/pages/Admin.tsx) (#289) | Reconcile layout (MetricCards + Members&access + All suites) |
| 11 | **SettingsScreen** (General·Secrets·Notifications·Danger) | `/settings` | ❌ **doesn't exist** | **New** (was Week-7, backend-gated) |
| 12 | ShareFlow (Share drawer) | drawer | ✅ [SharePanel.tsx](frontend/src/components/suites/SharePanel.tsx) | Stays a drawer ✓ (the *only* drawer) |
| 13 | **Error pages** (400–504 via `ErrorState`) | catch-all | 🟡 [ErrorBoundary.tsx](frontend/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx)/[Forbidden.tsx](frontend/src/components/Forbidden.tsx) partial | **New** full 4xx/5xx catalog |

### What the docs under-scoped (the gap you flagged)

The Week-6 list (`progress.md` 23 tasks) tracks the Dashboard, New-Suite page, edit-flows, results route, and error pages — but **three real screens aren't in it**:
- **Profile** — still a placeholder (`Home`), but the prototype has a full identity + alert-channels screen
- **Settings** — entirely new (General / Secrets / Notifications / Danger zone); was punted to Week 7 as "backend-gated"
- **Connection source-select split** — the prototype's two-page New-Connection flow isn't explicitly a task

So full Week-6 scope = the 23 tracked tasks **+** Profile content, Settings page, Admin-layout reconcile, and the connection source/config split.

### Out of scope (correctly)
Marketing landing page (post-v1), the **"View as" role switch** (prototype demo-only, not adopted), dark mode (post-v1, but the token contract is already dark-ready).

## The one structural decision the prototype is silent on

The whole `/dashboard` + Quality-Trends + per-check-trend work **gates on a chart library** — antd ships none, and `recharts` vs `@ant-design/charts` has real bundle-size + `pnpm audit` consequences. That's the ADR. Two calls to make before I draft it:

questions.question: Which charting library should the ADR adopt for the Dashboard + trend charts?

options.label: recharts (Recommended)
options.description: Lightweight, composable React-native SVG charts. ~95KB gz, MIT, clean audit history, lazy-loadable so it only ships on /dashboard. Best fit for our 4–5 chart types (bar trend, sparkline, progress).

options.label: @ant-design/charts
options.description: Official antd charting (G2Plot/G2 under the hood). Visually on-brand out of the box but much heavier (~300KB+ gz), larger dep tree → bigger audit/bundle surface.

options.label: Apache ECharts
options.description: Most powerful/feature-rich, canvas-based. Overkill for our needs and the largest footprint; better suited to heavy interactive dashboards.

options.label: Chart library only (Recommended)
options.description: One tight ADR for the chart-library decision (the gating call). The drawer→page restructure + scope expansion get captured in progress.md / CLAUDE.md, since they're prototype-adoption execution, not new architecture.

options.label: Chart lib + full prototype adoption
options.description: A broader ADR also formalizing the drawer→page flow restructure, the Settings/Admin pull-into-W6 scope expansion, and the screen inventory — a single Week-6 design-adoption record.

# ADR 0021 — Live test/demo-data environment (retail model) lives outside the repo

- **Status:** Accepted
- **Date:** 2026-06-21
- **Deciders:** @TheurgicDuke771
- **Related:** ADR [0004](0004-orchestration-abstraction.md) (orchestration abstraction — the flows exercise it), [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) / [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md) (Azure/Key Vault is one impl behind each seam; no Azure resource names in the repo), [0003](0003-gx-only-for-v1.md) (GX run paths the flows feed). Discharges the standing **"live warehouse/file run — deferred Week-1 smoke"** caveat carried in CLAUDE.md §13 and `docs/progress.md`.

## Context

Every DataQ run path — Snowflake (`SnowflakeCheckRunner`), flat-file (`FlatFileCheckRunner` + batch resolution), Unity Catalog (`UnityCatalogCheckRunner`) — is unit-tested against canned in-memory DataFrames with the **network seam stubbed** (real GX execution, mocked IO). That is deliberate and correct for CI, but it means no run has ever touched a live warehouse, live ADLS/S3 object, or live UC table. The roadmap has carried this as the *"deferred Week-1 smoke"* since Week 1.

Closing it needs a **realistic, repeatable data environment**: real datasources with believable data, real orchestration picking the data up, and the three DataQ run paths executing against it end-to-end. The data should model a real domain (retail) so checks, profiling, severity tiers and trends look like production, not toy fixtures.

The question this ADR settles is **what, if anything, of that environment belongs in the DataQ source repo.**

## Decision

**The test/demo-data environment is a harness that lives _outside_ the DataQ source repo.** None of the setup code is committed:

- **Terraform** that provisions Azure + Snowflake infra once accounts are enabled — local / separate, **not git-tracked** (also keeps Azure resource names out of the repo, per ADR 0010/0013).
- **Mock-data generators** for the retail model and the seed data they produce — out of repo.
- **The Databricks RAW→Silver→Gold notebook** that processes landed files — out of repo.

What **is** git-tracked is only documentation that points at the harness and records this decision: this ADR, plus the roadmap / `progress.md` pointers that finally give the deferred-smoke caveat an owner.

**Retail data model** the harness seeds (domains → entities): Product (style, colour, material, SKU) · Inventory (on-hand, planned) · Location (store, address, store hours) · Sales (placed, shipped, audited) · Return/Cancellation (return, cancellation, guest, platform) · Logistics (shipment, tracking, carrier) · Guest/Customer (details, survey, chatbot feedback). Plus cross-cutting: on-sale/discount, promotion/award/coupon, tax rate, exchange rate, product price, misc.

Data is split across **ADLS · Snowflake · Unity Catalog**; orchestration across **ADF · Airflow** (both behind the existing `OrchestrationProvider` abstraction — ADR 0004; no provider-specific branching).

**Three reference flows** the harness drives, each exercising a distinct DataQ run path:

- **Flow A — load to warehouse.** File lands in ADLS → ADF/Airflow picks it up → loads into Snowflake / UC → DataQ runs a suite against the warehouse table (`SnowflakeCheckRunner` / `UnityCatalogCheckRunner`).
- **Flow B — medallion.** File lands in UC RAW → Databricks notebook runs → processes RAW → Silver → Gold → DataQ runs suites against the UC tables.
- **Flow C — flat-file in place.** File lands in ADLS and **stays a flat file** (no warehouse load) → DataQ runs a suite directly against it (`FlatFileCheckRunner` + batch resolution picks the latest batch).

**Hosting — local/fast-iteration profile (chosen).** Airflow runs **locally via the official `apache/airflow` docker-compose** (webserver + scheduler + Postgres metadata DB + optional Celery workers), while **ADLS / Snowflake / Unity Catalog point at real cloud accounts**. This keeps the orchestration loop cheap and fast to iterate while the datasources stay realistic. Terraform is **not** used for the local Airflow (docker-compose is the norm; the `kreuzwerker/docker` provider is unnecessary friction); ADF, being managed, is exercised in its cloud form. A fully-cloud profile (Terraform-provisioned **Azure Managed Airflow** or AKS-hosted, alongside the rest of the infra) is a valid alternative for a more production-like run, but is **not** the v1 default. Either way Airflow stays an `OrchestrationProvider` behind the abstraction (ADR 0004) — DataQ is agnostic to how it is hosted.

## Consequences

**Positive**
- The deferred live-warehouse/file smoke gets a concrete, owned mechanism instead of floating as a caveat.
- The repo stays clean of infra/seed/harness code and Azure-specific resource names (ADR 0010/0013 anti-lock-in holds).
- The three flows give real end-to-end coverage of all three run paths **and** the ADF + Airflow orchestration trigger-on-success path (ADR 0004), against believable retail data.
- Profiler, severity tiers, and Week-6 trends/anomaly baselines get realistic data to render against.

**Negative / watch**
- The harness is unversioned alongside the product; its setup steps must be documented well enough to reproduce (README in the external harness location).
- **Boundary to hold:** if any harness artifact later becomes a *generic, user-facing reference template* (the way `integrations/airflow/dataq_airflow_callback.py` is a shipped template, **not** app code), it would move into `integrations/` (e.g. `integrations/databricks/`) and become git-tracked — but the retail-specific harness stays out. This ADR governs the demo harness, not future generic templates.

## Alternatives considered

- **Commit the harness into the repo (e.g. `scripts/mockdata/`, `infra/terraform/`).** Rejected: bloats the product repo with environment-specific seed + Azure resource names, working against the anti-lock-in posture (ADR 0010/0013), and couples product CI to harness churn. Credentials-in-tracked-files risk too (working agreement).
- **Keep relying on canned-frame unit tests only.** Rejected: never discharges the live smoke; profiling/trends keep running on toy data.
- **Ship the Databricks notebook as an `integrations/` template now.** Deferred: it's currently the retail-specific harness, not a generalised user template. Revisit if/when a generic medallion-integration reference is wanted.

1	# Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
2	
3	Each ADR captures a single significant architecture decision: the context, the decision, the consequences, and the alternatives considered. New ADRs are append-only — supersede an old decision by adding a new ADR and marking the old one's status as `Superseded by ADR-NNNN`.
4	
5	## Format
6	
7	- File name: `NNNN-short-kebab-slug.md` (zero-padded 4-digit sequence)
8	- Frontmatter fields: Status, Date, Deciders
9	- Sections: Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives considered, Related (optional)
10	- Keep each ADR short — 1–2 pages. If it grows past that, the decision is probably two decisions.
11	
12	## Index
13	
14	| # | Title | Status |
15	|---|---|---|
16	| [0001](0001-trunk-based-branching.md) | Trunk-based branching with squash-merge into `main` | Accepted |
17	| [0002](0002-conventional-commits.md) | Conventional commits for PR titles and commit messages | Accepted |
18	| [0003](0003-gx-only-for-v1.md) | GX-only for v1; DQX deferred to v1.1 for DLT/streaming | Accepted |
19	| [0004](0004-orchestration-abstraction.md) | Unified `OrchestrationProvider` abstraction for ADF and Airflow | Accepted |
20	| [0005](0005-severity-tier-weights.md) | Severity tier weights (warn / fail / critical → health score) | Accepted |
21	| [0006](0006-adf-webhook-authentication.md) | ADF webhook authentication (shared secret in URL + hard-cutover rotation) | Accepted |
22	| [0007](0007-airflow-callback-model.md) | Airflow callback model (HMAC-signed webhook + polling fallback) | Accepted |
23	| [0009](0009-flat-monorepo-layout.md) | Repo layout — flat monorepo (`backend/` + `frontend/`) | Accepted |
24	| [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) | Provider-agnostic infrastructure seams (Azure is the default, not the architecture) | Accepted |
25	| [0011](0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md) | Extensibility seams for deferred connectors and integrations | Accepted |
26	| [0012](0012-monitor-kind-seam.md) | Monitor-kind seam (`check.kind` discriminator + numeric metric storage) | Accepted |
27	| [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md) | Marketplace distribution (customer-deployed BYOL) and anti-vendor-lock-in guardrails | Accepted |
28	| [0014](0014-reconciliation-comparison-check-kind.md) | Cross-dataset reconciliation as a `comparison` check kind (reuse FastAPI_DataComparison engine) | Accepted |

32	| [0019](0019-custom-sql-check-kind.md) | Custom-SQL checks ride `kind='expectation'` via GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation` (no new kind); read-only validation + SQL-datasource gating | Accepted |

34	| [0021](0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md) | Live test/demo-data environment (retail model, 3 reference flows) lives outside the repo — Terraform/mock-data/Databricks notebook not git-tracked; discharges the deferred live-warehouse/file smoke | Accepted |

# Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)

Each ADR captures a single significant architecture decision: the context, the decision, the consequences, and the alternatives considered. New ADRs are append-only — supersede an old decision by adding a new ADR and marking the old one's status as `Superseded by ADR-NNNN`.

## Format

- File name: `NNNN-short-kebab-slug.md` (zero-padded 4-digit sequence)
- Frontmatter fields: Status, Date, Deciders
- Sections: Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives considered, Related (optional)
- Keep each ADR short — 1–2 pages. If it grows past that, the decision is probably two decisions.

## Index

| # | Title | Status |
|---|---|---|
| [0001](0001-trunk-based-branching.md) | Trunk-based branching with squash-merge into `main` | Accepted |
| [0002](0002-conventional-commits.md) | Conventional commits for PR titles and commit messages | Accepted |
| [0003](0003-gx-only-for-v1.md) | GX-only for v1; DQX deferred to v1.1 for DLT/streaming | Accepted |
| [0004](0004-orchestration-abstraction.md) | Unified `OrchestrationProvider` abstraction for ADF and Airflow | Accepted |
| [0005](0005-severity-tier-weights.md) | Severity tier weights (warn / fail / critical → health score) | Accepted |
| [0006](0006-adf-webhook-authentication.md) | ADF webhook authentication (shared secret in URL + hard-cutover rotation) | Accepted |
| [0007](0007-airflow-callback-model.md) | Airflow callback model (HMAC-signed webhook + polling fallback) | Accepted |
| [0009](0009-flat-monorepo-layout.md) | Repo layout — flat monorepo (`backend/` + `frontend/`) | Accepted |
| [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) | Provider-agnostic infrastructure seams (Azure is the default, not the architecture) | Accepted |
| [0011](0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md) | Extensibility seams for deferred connectors and integrations | Accepted |
| [0012](0012-monitor-kind-seam.md) | Monitor-kind seam (`check.kind` discriminator + numeric metric storage) | Accepted |
| [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md) | Marketplace distribution (customer-deployed BYOL) and anti-vendor-lock-in guardrails | Accepted |
| [0014](0014-reconciliation-comparison-check-kind.md) | Cross-dataset reconciliation as a `comparison` check kind (reuse FastAPI_DataComparison engine) | Accepted |
| [0016](0016-severity-derivation-semantics.md) | Severity derivation semantics (band the unexpected-%, thresholds override GX success) | Accepted |
| [0017](0017-python-313-runtime-upgrade.md) | Upgrade Python runtime 3.11 → 3.13 (3.14 deferred — GX-capped); bundled with the Snowflake 3→4 CVE refresh | Accepted |
| [0018](0018-results-surface-and-grafana-deferral.md) | Results surface is an in-app page (suite-scoped authz + PII redaction); Grafana deferred to optional ops add-on | Accepted |
| [0019](0019-custom-sql-check-kind.md) | Custom-SQL checks ride `kind='expectation'` via GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation` (no new kind); read-only validation + SQL-datasource gating | Accepted |
| [0020](0020-history-and-audit-strategy.md) | History/audit: per-entity Type-4 snapshot tables (`check_versions`, `connection_versions`) where config history is needed; no SCD-2; credentials never snapshotted; cascade-delete accepted; cross-entity audit log deferred | Accepted |
| [0021](0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md) | Live test/demo-data environment (retail model, 3 reference flows) lives outside the repo — Terraform/mock-data/Databricks notebook not git-tracked; discharges the deferred live-warehouse/file smoke | Accepted |

## Pending (to be written in their respective weeks)

|---|---|---|

| 0015 | Two-connection check model (source + target refs for `comparison` checks) | When reconciliation build starts (post-v1) |

# ADR 0022 — Week-6 prototype adoption (dedicated-page flows + full screen set) and chart library (recharts)

- **Status:** Accepted
- **Date:** 2026-06-21
- **Deciders:** @TheurgicDuke771
- **Related:** ADR [0018](0018-results-surface-and-grafana-deferral.md) (results is an in-app page, not Grafana — this ADR builds its dashboard/charts), [0005](0005-severity-tier-weights.md) / [0016](0016-severity-derivation-semantics.md) (the severity/status model the screens render), [0011](0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md) (the `ResultPublisher` alerting seam Profile/Settings notification UI fronts), [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) / [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md) (no Azure resource names baked into the new Settings/Secrets screen — Key Vault is one impl behind the seam). Tracked in epic [#175](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/175).

## Context

Week 6 ("Results dashboard + alerting") is also the week we adopt the **DataQ Design System** prototype (claude.ai/design project *"DataQ Design System"*, `projectId 317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d`; its `readme.md` is the spec). The prototype was reverse-engineered from the shipped indigo/antd app, so the **foundation already matches** (`theme.ts` palette, `resultsFormat.ts` status model, the 56px-header/220px-sider shell) — this is **not a re-skin**. It adopts the prototype's **structure**.

Two problems pushed this to an ADR:

1. **The Week-6 task list under-scoped the work.** `progress.md` tracked 23 tasks but the prototype's `templates/app/index.html` registers **13 screens**, and three real screens fell out of the tracking: **Profile** (still a placeholder `Home` component), **Settings** (didn't exist; had been punted to Week 7 as "backend-gated"), and the **New-Connection source-select split**. The decision now is to build the **full prototype screen set in Week 6**, not a subset.
2. **The dashboard + trend charts gate on a charting library** the prototype is silent about (it fakes charts with divs). antd ships none, so this is a real dependency decision with bundle-size + `pnpm audit` consequences.

## Decision

### A. Adopt the full prototype screen set in Week 6

Every create/edit flow becomes a **dedicated, deep-linkable page**; **the Share drawer is the _only_ surviving drawer**. Where the prototype differs from the shipped app, **the prototype wins**. The 13 screens and their disposition:

| Screen | Route | Disposition |
|---|---|---|
| AppShell / nav | — | Restructure: sider → **Dashboard · Connections · Suites · Results · Profile**; footer group **Admin · Settings · Documentation**; login redirect `/` → `/dashboard` |
| **Dashboard** | `/dashboard` | **New** landing — KPI cards, Quality Trends chart, Suite Performance bars, Recent Runs table (gated on §B) |
| Connections | `/connections` | Exists — align |
| New Connection | `/connections/new` → source-select → config | Adopt the **categorized source-select** step (Orchestration first), then spec-driven config |
| Suites | `/suites` | Exists — align (two-panel) |
| **New Suite** | `/suites/new` | **Restructure** `SuiteDrawer` create → page |
| Add Check | `/suites/:id/checks/new` | Exists — align |
| **Edit flows** | `/connections/:id/edit`, `/suites/:id/edit`, `/suites/:id/checks/:checkId/edit` | **Restructure** the Connection/Suite/Check **edit drawers** → pages reusing the create page + prefill |
| Results | `/results` | Exists — add KPI cards + filter expansion |
| **Run detail** | `/results/:runId` | **Restructure** the in-page `openRun` drawer → routed page; add CSV/JSON download |
| **Profile** | `/profile` | **New** real content (identity + alert-channel toggles) — replaces the placeholder `Home` |
| Admin | `/admin` | Exists (#289) — reconcile layout to the prototype (MetricCards + Members & access + All suites) |
| **Settings** | `/settings` | **New** — General · Secrets (Key Vault) · Notifications · Danger zone. **Pulled into Week 6** (was Week 7) |
| Share | drawer | Stays a drawer (the only one) ✓ |
| **Error pages** | router catch-all + `ErrorBoundary` fallback | **New** `ErrorState`-based 400/401/403/404/429/500/502/503/504 catalog |

**Built with real Ant Design**, reading tokens from `theme.ts`. The prototype's framework-free `.jsx` recreations and `_ds_bundle.js` are **reference only** (per the design `readme.md`).

**Honesty constraints carried forward:**
- Render **only backend-backed KPIs** on the Dashboard/Results (no invented metrics — the W7 "KPI honesty pass"). Where the prototype shows a metric with no backend (e.g. "Avg. Time to Resolution"), either wire it or omit it — don't fake it.
- Failing-row **sample drill-down stays withheld** pending row-level PII redaction ([#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226), ADR 0018).
- Settings **Secrets** tab shows Key Vault state via the generic secret seam — **no hardcoded Azure resource names** in component/business logic (ADR 0010/0013).
- Notification toggles (Profile/Settings) front the **`ResultPublisher`** seam (ADR 0011); Teams is the v1 impl.

### B. Chart library: **recharts**

Adopt **recharts** for the Dashboard KPI/trend charts and the per-check historical trend. Rationale: lightweight composable React-native SVG (~95 KB gz), MIT, clean audit history, and **lazy-loaded** so it ships only on chart-bearing routes (`/dashboard`, run/check detail) — keeping the initial bundle lean, consistent with the existing code-split routes in `App.tsx`. It clears the `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` + bundle-size merge gate.

### Explicitly NOT adopted
- **"View as" role switch** (prototype user-menu demo only) — real authz is server-driven via `/me` (#289).
- **Marketing landing page** (`templates/marketing/`) — post-v1.
- **Dark mode** — post-v1; the semantic-token contract already keeps the app dark-ready (`tokens/dark.css`), so no work now.
- The prototype `_ds_bundle.js` / framework-free component recreations — reference only.

## Consequences

**Positive**
- One honest, complete Week-6 scope: every shipped screen has a prototype-matched target, and the three under-tracked screens (Profile, Settings, connection source-select) are now first-class.
- Deep-linkable, refreshable pages for every flow (drawers were not) — better URLs, back-nav, and browser history. Only Share stays modal.
- recharts unblocks the Dashboard and the per-check trend without a heavy dependency; lazy-loading contains its bundle cost.

**Negative / watch**
- **Scope expansion**: pulling Settings + Admin-reconcile into Week 6 grows the week beyond the original roadmap. Tracked honestly in `progress.md` (W6 task count rises; CLAUDE.md §13 headline updated).
- New runtime dependency (recharts) enters the `pnpm audit` + Dependabot surface — acceptable, but it's one more thing to keep patched.
- Removing the create/edit **drawers** touches `Connections.tsx` / `Suites.tsx` / the drawer components; must dedup with the open frontend nits ([#204](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/204)) and keep the extracted form specs (`connectionFormSpec.ts` / `checkForm.ts` / `suiteTarget.ts`) as the shared source for both create and edit.
- Settings/Secrets/Admin surfaces touch sensitive config — hold the anti-lock-in line (no Azure names in logic) and the KPI-honesty line (no metric without a backing query).

## Alternatives considered

- **`@ant-design/charts`** — visually on-brand out of the box, but G2Plot/G2 underneath makes it ~300 KB+ gz with a much larger dep/audit tree. Rejected: weight out' weighs the brand-fit gain for our 4–5 simple chart types.
- **Apache ECharts** — most powerful, canvas-based; overkill and the largest footprint. Rejected for v1.
- **Build the subset (just Dashboard + Results route) in Week 6, defer Profile/Settings/Admin to Week 7.** Rejected: that is exactly the under-scoping this ADR corrects — the screens are cohesive (shared shell, nav, error pages), and splitting them fragments the adoption.
- **Keep create/edit as drawers, only add new pages.** Rejected: the prototype's structural decision is dedicated pages everywhere except Share; partial adoption leaves an inconsistent navigation model.

# Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)

Each ADR captures a single significant architecture decision: the context, the decision, the consequences, and the alternatives considered. New ADRs are append-only — supersede an old decision by adding a new ADR and marking the old one's status as `Superseded by ADR-NNNN`.

## Format

- File name: `NNNN-short-kebab-slug.md` (zero-padded 4-digit sequence)
- Frontmatter fields: Status, Date, Deciders
- Sections: Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives considered, Related (optional)
- Keep each ADR short — 1–2 pages. If it grows past that, the decision is probably two decisions.

## Index

| # | Title | Status |
|---|---|---|
| [0001](0001-trunk-based-branching.md) | Trunk-based branching with squash-merge into `main` | Accepted |
| [0002](0002-conventional-commits.md) | Conventional commits for PR titles and commit messages | Accepted |
| [0003](0003-gx-only-for-v1.md) | GX-only for v1; DQX deferred to v1.1 for DLT/streaming | Accepted |
| [0004](0004-orchestration-abstraction.md) | Unified `OrchestrationProvider` abstraction for ADF and Airflow | Accepted |
| [0005](0005-severity-tier-weights.md) | Severity tier weights (warn / fail / critical → health score) | Accepted |
| [0006](0006-adf-webhook-authentication.md) | ADF webhook authentication (shared secret in URL + hard-cutover rotation) | Accepted |
| [0007](0007-airflow-callback-model.md) | Airflow callback model (HMAC-signed webhook + polling fallback) | Accepted |
| [0009](0009-flat-monorepo-layout.md) | Repo layout — flat monorepo (`backend/` + `frontend/`) | Accepted |
| [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) | Provider-agnostic infrastructure seams (Azure is the default, not the architecture) | Accepted |
| [0011](0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md) | Extensibility seams for deferred connectors and integrations | Accepted |
| [0012](0012-monitor-kind-seam.md) | Monitor-kind seam (`check.kind` discriminator + numeric metric storage) | Accepted |
| [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md) | Marketplace distribution (customer-deployed BYOL) and anti-vendor-lock-in guardrails | Accepted |
| [0014](0014-reconciliation-comparison-check-kind.md) | Cross-dataset reconciliation as a `comparison` check kind (reuse FastAPI_DataComparison engine) | Accepted |
| [0016](0016-severity-derivation-semantics.md) | Severity derivation semantics (band the unexpected-%, thresholds override GX success) | Accepted |
| [0017](0017-python-313-runtime-upgrade.md) | Upgrade Python runtime 3.11 → 3.13 (3.14 deferred — GX-capped); bundled with the Snowflake 3→4 CVE refresh | Accepted |
| [0018](0018-results-surface-and-grafana-deferral.md) | Results surface is an in-app page (suite-scoped authz + PII redaction); Grafana deferred to optional ops add-on | Accepted |
| [0019](0019-custom-sql-check-kind.md) | Custom-SQL checks ride `kind='expectation'` via GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation` (no new kind); read-only validation + SQL-datasource gating | Accepted |
| [0020](0020-history-and-audit-strategy.md) | History/audit: per-entity Type-4 snapshot tables (`check_versions`, `connection_versions`) where config history is needed; no SCD-2; credentials never snapshotted; cascade-delete accepted; cross-entity audit log deferred | Accepted |
| [0021](0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md) | Live test/demo-data environment (retail model, 3 reference flows) lives outside the repo — Terraform/mock-data/Databricks notebook not git-tracked; discharges the deferred live-warehouse/file smoke | Accepted |

## Pending (to be written in their respective weeks)

| # | Topic | Target week |
|---|---|---|
| 0008 | MCP mounted at `/mcp` with Azure AD auth | Week 7 |
| 0015 | Two-connection check model (source + target refs for `comparison` checks) | When reconciliation build starts (post-v1) |
